concerns.text: FR-005 mandates computation of degree, betweenness, and eigenvector centrality for functional connectivity and performs group comparisons. The original idea only required mediation analysis of *structural* centrality of DMN hubs linking functional connectivity strength to ASD severity; functional centrality metrics and group comparisons were never requested, constituting an over‑reach beyond the stated research question.

FR-006 specifies non‑parametric permutation tests and FDR correction for functional centrality group comparisons. Since functional centrality analysis is not part of the original hypothesis, this requirement adds unnecessary methodological work.

FR-007 defines a correlation‑thresholding step for binary adjacency matrices used in functional centrality analysis, which again is unrelated to the mediation hypothesis and expands scope beyond the idea.

FR-011 requires a sensitivity sweep over correlation thresholds for functional graphs. This analysis is tied to the functional centrality pipeline that was not requested in the idea, representing further scope creep.
